body.title: Pre-execution policy gate in BaseShim: allow/deny before dispatch
body.result: PolicyGate in BaseShim.dispatch(): provenance+allowed_actions+budget checks, governance JSONL log, AgentContext/_policy param, 26 tests green
body.description:
The shim already intercepts all tool calls (T-shim-traffic-spy adds logging). Extend it: before dispatch, evaluate the call against policy. Allow or deny. Turns shim from observer into gatekeeper.

Policy check order: (1) provenance token valid? (T-provenance-identity); (2) action in agent profile allowed_actions?; (3) budget remaining? (T-inference-budget-ledger). Any check fails → deny, log decision with reason, return error to caller.

Every governance decision (allow or deny) writes a structured record: agent_id, action, policy_checked, verdict, reason, ts. This is the 'decision bill of materials' — a full audit chain of what was allowed and why, not just what happened.

Policy device (new, lightweight): stores allowed_actions rules per agent_type. Rules are declarative YAML, loaded from config/policies/. Shim calls policy device synchronously before dispatch — must be fast (<5ms).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/skeleton/base_shim.py, devices/policy/ (new), config/policies/ (new), datacenter_logs/shim/trace/ (governance decisions appended here)
**Design rules:** BaseDevice/BaseShim; policy device is a dependency of shim — must boot before any device registers; flat-file policies survive DB outage
**Scope boundary:** IN — pre-execution allow/deny, decision bill of materials, policy YAML per agent_type, provenance+profile+budget checks; OUT — content inspection of payloads, output validation (that is a separate post-result hook)
**Completion criteria:** a tool call from an agent without a valid provenance token is denied and logged; a tool call exceeding budget is denied; a tool call not in allowed_actions is denied; all denials appear in decision log with reason; allowed calls proceed with <5ms added latency
